title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs bisect, reproducible diagnosis, and automated regression search from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reproducible bisect harness and causal report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Bisect Search Invariant · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G12 is a review in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

Which action demonstrates Gita Bisect Search Invariant without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Bisect tests commits in timestamp order and always needs exactly log2(n) trials.

lines.label: evidence

steps.title: Record what required effort

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
